assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: In plan.md Step 4 ('Understand the Code'), the plan explicitly lists inspecting: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' — 'Would use mcp__serena_backend__get_symbols_overview to see existing methods (fetch, list, search)', (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs' — 'GET handler pattern (Path extraction, service call, JSON response)', (3) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs' — 'AdvisorySummary struct (has severity field used for counting)'. Three of the four specified files are referenced for inspection before modification.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6-7 lists exactly 6 files matching the task scope: 3 to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint, advisory_summary.rs tests) and 3 to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs). Step 9 ('Scope Containment') explicitly validates via 'git diff --name-only' against declared files. The plan mentions docs/api.md as potentially needing updates but explicitly flags it: 'If docs/api.md was modified, it would be flagged as out-of-scope and the user would be asked to approve.' No unrelated files are included in the planned modifications.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6-7 'Files to Create (3 files)' lists: (1) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs — SeveritySummary response struct', (2) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs — GET handler', (3) 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs — Integration tests'. Each has a corresponding detailed description file (file-1-description.md, file-2-description.md, file-3-description.md) with full implementation details.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 6-7 'Files to Modify (3 files)' lists: (4) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs — Register new model module', (5) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs — Register new route', (6) 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs — Add severity_summary method'. Each has a corresponding detailed description file (file-4-description.md, file-5-description.md, file-6-description.md).

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 shows the commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' — this follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope '(advisory)', and description. The footer contains 'Implements TC-9201', referencing the Jira key.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 10 states: "With trailer: `--trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code'`"

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 5 shows: 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201' and states 'Branch name: TC-9201, based off target branch main.' The target branch 'main' is parsed in Step 1 ('Target Branch: main') and used as the base for branch creation and as the PR base in Step 10 ('gh pr create --base main').

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md identifies both patterns explicitly: (1) Error Handling section: 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> where AppError is defined in common/src/error.rs. Errors are wrapped with .context() for descriptive error messages.' (2) Module Structure section: 'Each domain module follows a strict model/ + service/ + endpoints/ structure under modules/fundamental/src/<domain>/.' Additional patterns are also identified including endpoint patterns, response types, naming conventions, and database patterns.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1 ('Fetch and Parse Jira Task') explicitly lists 'Target Branch: main' as a parsed section. This value is then used in Step 5 ('based off target branch main'), and in Step 10 ('gh pr create --base main').

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: Plan.md Step 1.5 ('Verify Description Integrity') states: 'No digest comment was found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced.' Followed by: '> Warning: No description digest found -- skipping integrity check (backward compatibility).' This matches the expected behavior of proceeding with a warning rather than blocking.
